text: Last summer, I spent months planning a trip to Japan. Booking flights, researching neighborhoods, and learning a few basic phrases were all part of the process. Once there, the new cultures amazed me: from the quiet order of train stations to the polite bows exchanged between strangers. Food was a highlight. I still remember the warmth of a bowl of ramen on a rainy night, and the surprise of finding savory flavors in what I thought would be a sweet dessert. However, language barriers created some awkward moments, like ordering the wrong dish or getting lost in a small alley. Despite the challenges, those memories—both the funny and the beautiful—are unforgettable.
